purpose: Industry rollups for cyber. Each industry names jobs, raw inputs, processed outputs, and seasonal cadence. Used by the world-economy heartbeat to model price + scarcity + production cycles. The dominant economic substrate is information itself — every other industry is downstream of who owns which data.

industries.seasonal_peak: Frostgrid (audit season — corps buy clean dossiers on each other)
industries.notes: Top-3% of brokers earn more in a month than a fantasy world's entire annual royal treasury. The trade has no physical product, no inventory, no tax footprint the corps can pin down.

cross_world_exports_to: superhero (the bio_powers + chrome stack is the most lucrative cross-world arbitrage)
industries.notes: Three quality tiers: corp_certified (insurance covers, 1-year warranty), gray_market (no warranty, 6-month MTBF), back-alley (no anaesthesia, 50% survival rate). All three are legal — Grid Authority taxes per-procedure regardless of quality.
The largest single industry by headcount. Every corp employs 1 sec personnel per 8 office workers. Pay is mid-tier but benefits include corp citizenship and aug-credit.
70% of surface_slum employment. No benefits, no citizenship, paid per task. Drones are slowly displacing the human end of this market. Gig union (the Static Collective) is a major political force.

cross_world_exports_to: tunya (back-alley channel for tunyan dyes laundered through cyber)
industries.notes: Operates in tacitly-tolerated zones. Corp Grid Authority taxes 12% of gross — even gray markets pay tax.

ICE designers are paid in the top 1% citywide. Kill-construct work is technically extralegal but every major corp runs at least one. Crossing an ice_architect's product is the fastest way to wake up in a body-bag.

industries.seasonal_peak: Smog (07) — month-long aug-clinic peak as respiratory implants spike
cross_world_exports_to: superhero (cyber aug-installation is the gold standard)
industries.notes: Three tiers: corp-clinic (sterile, expensive, slow), street-clinic (cheap, fast, ~85% success), gray-clinic (back-alley, illegal procedures only, no records).

industries.seasonal_peak: Dark Hours (24:00-28:00 daily — when corp sysadmins are off-shift)
industries.notes: Cyber's signature exotic profession. ~3% of deckers retire wealthy; ~40% die in the chair before age 35 from neural backlash, kill-construct strikes, or corp wetwork. The trade has the highest pay-per-hour and the highest mortality-per-hour of any profession in any of the 9 worlds.
